title: Disposable multi-instance demo stacks
acceptance: M3-D5: one demo stack (postgres+redis) isolated alongside the dev anthropos stack — up/status/down, dev untouched. Verified live on a 16 GB box.

decisions.M3-D3: clone at latest release tag, not main
decisions.M3-D4: tooling home = own gitignored anthropos-demo/demo-stacks repo; skills+guide in rosetta
decisions.M3-D5: acceptance adjusted to 1 stack alongside dev (16 GB host can't fit 2 full stacks)

injection_status: authn + clerk-frontend BUILT + PROVEN (authn: vendored disarmed colony accepts a Clerkenstein token, proven against colony v0.34.3 = app's pinned version; frontend: publishable-key mint format-identical to the gated impl). clerk-backend/clerk-webhook: artifacts emitted, verified in the running-app deployment. The patched-colony module now EXISTS (demo-stacks/inject/) — earlier 'doesn't exist' was wrong.
resource_gated: MEASURED FALSE: the whole dev stack uses ~0.9 GB (not 10-12). RAM is NOT a blocker. The only remaining piece is the full running-app Docker deployment (per-demo mirror build) — integration, not feasibility.
carried_forward: M3-CF1 — the running-app deployment proof: build a demo app image with the vendored disarmed colony, bring it up, confirm a live request with a Clerkenstein token is accepted end-to-end (+ clerk-backend cert/redirect + frontend rebuild). The injection mechanism is already proven at the Go level.
dev_stack_safety: every op -p demo-N scoped; down hard-refuses the dev project; verified the dev stack (12 containers) stayed intact across demo-1's lifecycle AND the failed app-bring-up attempt
